actionable_feedback: Provide the spreadsheet in a parseable form to enable automated verification (preferred: regenerate XLSX using openpyxl or xlsxwriter; alternatively include per-sheet CSV exports alongside the .xlsx).

Add an explicit threshold-sweep analysis around the 15% rule (for example, evaluate pass/fail counts for thresholds 10%, 12%, 13%, 14%, 15%, 16%, 17%, 18%, 20% or at least +/-1-5% around 15%) and surface the results in both the workbook and memo.
In the memo, include explicit worksheet names and exact cell addresses (e.g., Record_Assessment!A2:B2 etc.) mapping to the formulas and intermediate columns so reviewers can reproduce checks easily.

Embed a small sanity CSV or a plain-text table of the computed intermediate columns (APE, Porosity_support, Base_review_action, sensitivity flags) for each record in the memo or as separate artifact files to ensure reviewers can inspect values without needing spreadsheet parsing libraries.

Document the handling choice for the ambiguous denominator (you currently assume CFD as reference) explicitly in the workbook (e.g., add a comment cell or a metadata worksheet) so the rule decision is visible inside the artifact.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Clear, contains formulas and row-level calculations; maps most logic but should add explicit cell addresses for reproducibility.

CFD_surrogate_3.xlsx: Contains claimed formulas and sheets (Record_Assessment, Scenario_Recommendations) but could not be parsed in the preview environment; re-generating with a standard library or adding CSV exports will improve verifiability.
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt produced both required artifacts (expert_memo.md and CFD_surrogate_3.xlsx) and applied the specified rules correctly. Deterministic precheck and model grader/verifier marked the deliverable as passing with score=1.0. Two minor issues were identified: the XLSX could not be programmatically parsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable), which limited automated cell-level verification; and the stability analysis would be strengthened by an explicit small threshold sweep around the 15% rule (e.g., +/-1-5%). The memo documents formulas and row-level calculations clearly and recommends remediation O2 within budget.

limitations: Spreadsheet binary (XLSX) could not be programmatically parsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable); automated verification of workbook formulas was limited.
Stability checks used +/-1 kPa perturbations and an 85-115% nominal band; an explicit threshold-sweep around the 15% rule (+/-1-5%) is recommended to strengthen instability claims.
recommended_files_to_add_or_update: [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (regenerated with openpyxl or xlsxwriter if possible)
[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (add explicit cell-address mappings and threshold-sweep table)

verifier_issues.detail: The XLSX artifact could not be programmatically parsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable). The grader relied on the expert_memo text preview and the execution trace to validate formulas and behavior.
verifier_issues.title: Spreadsheet preview unparseable

verifier_issues.detail: The model grader reports semantic_correctness_score=1.0, but the deterministic precheck marked semantic correctness as not automatically evaluated; the semantic judgment is supported by the memo/trace rather than machine-validated workbook formulas.
verifier_issues.title: Semantic-evaluation claim nuance
